prompt: Operator: BatchNormV3 training-mode forward. x [N,C,L]=[32,64,64] float32; weight/bias [C] float32; eps=1e-5. Per channel reduce over N and L: mean=sum/(N*L), var=sumsq/(N*L)-mean^2, invstd=1/sqrt(var+eps); out=x*scale+shift with scale=weight*invstd, shift=bias-scale*mean. Outputs out [N,C,L], saveMean [C], saveInvStd [C]. Inputs MUST be torch.Tensor (C310 zeroes numpy for this reduction pattern). On-chip strided reduction (NO host transpose): view x as [N*C, L]; CORE_NUM=8, ch=8 channels/core; per sample n load a [ch, L] strided tile at row n*C+c0 and accumulate `asc2.reduce_sum(blk, 1)` -> [ch] per-channel partials (2D reduce over the L axis is exact; a 3D [1,ch,L] reduce over axis 2 silently fills only channel 0 -- avoid it). Use asc2.rsqrt on the [ch] var tile; broadcast scale/shift with `asc2.broadcast_to(asc2.expand_dims(scale, 1), ch, L)` (broadcast_to takes VARARG dims, not a list). Tile-shape dims must be literal ints (module constants N_SIZE/C_SIZE/L_SIZE/CH), NOT ConstExpr. HONEST PERF NOTE: this strided per-channel reduction is DMA/instruction-bound (~62588 ticks vs 6110 aclnnBatchNorm, ratio ~0.10); the goal here is a correct vector-only realization, not beating the reference. Tolerance: mean/invstd 1e-3, out 1e-2 vs torch fp64. Platform: Ascend950PR_9599. Build/run: python3.11 kernel.py -r Model -v Ascend950PR_9599. Workaround: Reference golden/kernels/batch_norm_v3_f32.py for the exact channel-vectorized strided-reduce + broadcast affine structure.
